=== bd1989-07-20_atlantic_city.txt
===================
===================
===================
Bally's Grand Hotel 	
Atlantic City, New Jersey
20 July 1989



                         1.  Most Likely You Go Your Way (And I'll Go Mine)
                         2.  Lenny Bruce (with harp)
                         3.  John Brown
                         4.  I Want You (Dylan on acoustic guitar and harp)
                         5.  You're A Big Girl Now (Dylan on acoustic guitar and harp)
                         6.  Highway 61 Revisited(G. E. Smith on electric slide guitar,  Dylan on acoustic guitar)
                         7.  Mama, You Been On My Mind (with harp)
                         8.  It's Alright, Ma (I'm Only Bleeding)


                         1.  Boots Of Spanish Leather (with harp)
                         2.  Silvio (Bob Dylan/Robert Hunter)
                         3.  Leopard-Skin Pill-Box Hat
                         4.  Legend In My Time (Don Gibson) (with harp)
                         5.  In The Garden
                         6.  Like A Rolling Stone
                         7.  It Ain't Me, Babe (with harp)
                         8.  Maggie's Farm

Concert # 107 of The Never-Ending Tour.  Concert # 15 of the 1989 USA Summer Tour.
Concert # 29 with the second Never-Ending Tour Band: Bob Dylan (vocal & guitar), G. E. Smith (guitar), Tony Garnier (bass), Christopher Parker (drums).

4-6  Bob Dylan on acoustic guitar.
7-9 and 15 Bob Dylan (vocal & guitar), G.E. Smith (guitar).
2, 4, 5, 7, 9, 15  Bob Dylan harmonica.
6  G. E. Smith (electric slide guitar).
10  G. E. Smith (backup vocal).

Note. First performance of Lenny Bruce during The Never-Ending Tour.

12 new songs (75%) compared to previous concert. 2 new songs for this tour.

information lifted from www.bjorner.com/bob and my own onotes

This i smost likely from same source as LB-2217, same disc-split and same low levels at start. However, as that doesn't state any lineage, I don't know which one has less generation. This one was received in snail-mail trade -> flac via eac -> TLH -> you
